# Anti-Patterns — Map Failures

**Anti-Pattern 1: Map Rigidity. Refusing to update your map when anomalies appear.**

**Anti-Pattern 2: Map Chaos. Having no map at all. Existential confusion.**

**Case: The Ideologue. Someone whose map is impervious to evidence. Dangerously rigid.**

**Case: The Cynic. Someone who has rejected all maps. Destruction.**

**Case: The Comfort Seeker. Someone who avoids chaos entirely. No growth.**

**Case: The Denier. Someone who pretends chaos does not exist. Fatal.**

**Case: The Resentment. Resentment is the refusal to accept the map's failure.**
**Case: The Tyranny. Imposing your map on others by force.**
**Case: The Chaos. Too much chaos destroys the ability to act.**
**Case: The Certainty. Too much certainty prevents growth.**
**Case: The Denial. Avoiding anomalies makes the map brittle.**
**Case: The Totalitarianism. Attempting to eliminate chaos entirely creates tyranny.**
**Case: The Nihilism. Believing nothing matters makes meaningful action impossible.**
**Case: The Dogma. Accepting a map without examining it is dangerous.**
**Case: The Rebellion. Rejecting a map without understanding it is also dangerous.**
**Case: The Polarization. Refusing to integrate opposites leads to extremism.**
**Case: The Inertia. Staying in the known too long leads to stagnation.**
